title: Israeli Drone Kills 9-Year-Old Adel Al-Najjar in Eastern Khan Younis
detail: An Israeli drone strike killed 9-year-old Adel Al-Najjar in eastern Khan Younis on April 28, 2026, while the child was reportedly collecting cardboard for cooking fuel. The killing drew sharp international condemnation. The IDF stated it was targeting a 'terror operative' near the Yellow Line and that the incident was under review. Separately, four other Palestinians — including a senior Hamas intelligence operative — were killed in an Israeli airstrike on the Al-Rimal neighborhood of Gaza City. The day's casualties brought the total killed since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire to approximately 824 Palestinians, with 2,318 wounded. The UN Security Council held an emergency briefing the same day at which ASG Khiari described the ceasefire as 'increasingly fragile.' Euronews confirmed five total killed in Israeli strikes on April 28, including the child.

media.caption: At least five Palestinians killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza, including 9-year-old Adel Al-Najjar in Khan Younis, April 28, 2026

title: UN Security Council: Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile' — 824 Palestinians Killed Since October 2025
detail: UN Assistant Secretary-General Khaled Khiari briefed the Security Council on April 28, 2026, characterizing the Gaza ceasefire as 'increasingly fragile' and outlining an acute deterioration in humanitarian conditions. Key figures from the briefing: 824 Palestinians killed and 2,318 wounded since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire; 40 Palestinians killed in the single week of April 20–27; 1.8 million people (nearly the entire Gaza population) displaced and living in IDP conditions with collapsed essential services. Khiari noted that armed activities by both sides continued, with Israeli strikes ongoing and Palestinian armed groups maintaining a presence. Tony Blair also addressed the Council on reconstruction planning under the Board of Peace framework. The briefing occurred as WHO reported 92% of Gaza's housing has been destroyed or damaged and the OHCHR characterized the continuous displacement of civilians as amounting to 'forcible transfer' under international law.

media.caption: UN Security Council hears Gaza ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile' as 824 Palestinians killed since October 2025

title: Israeli Settlers Burn Palestinian Home in Jalud; IDF Stands By — Pattern of Ceasefire-Era Settler Violence
detail: Israeli settlers burned a Palestinian family's home in Jalud village (south of Nablus) on April 28, 2026, beating an elderly man in the attack while Israeli soldiers stood by without intervening. CNN and the Jerusalem Post reported that approximately 12 settlers were involved; only one was arrested. The incident was part of an escalating pattern of settler violence documented during the post-ceasefire period: CNN's reporting noted ongoing water access sabotage by settlers in the West Bank, with OCHA recording 925 movement obstacles — the highest total in over 20 years, 43% above the two-decade average. WAFA separately reported a father and son were seriously injured in a settler attack near Adh-Dhahiriya, south of Hebron, the same day. The Jerusalem Post reported the IDF's inaction during the settler attack as contributing to the breakdown of Palestinian community security structures in the northern West Bank.

media.caption: Settlers burn Palestinian home in Jalud while IDF stands by; CNN reports pattern of settler water sabotage in West Bank, April 28, 2026
